paragraphs.text: Sunlight does not descend uniformly. Each wavelength is filtered, absorbed, and scattered at a different rate. By 200 meters, the visible spectrum has collapsed to a faint, monochrome blue — and beyond, only darkness, broken solely by bioluminescent code.

Water acts as a selective filter. Long-wavelength reds are absorbed within meters of the surface; cooler, shorter wavelengths persist deeper, until only a narrow band of blue-green photons reach the threshold of the dysphotic zone at 200 meters.

Of the full electromagnetic spectrum, only a narrow visible band ever reaches beyond surface waters. Below, the visible strip is rendered, then masked through an SVG <clipPath> to isolate the 470–700 nm range that meaningfully penetrates seawater.
An interactive infographic atlas of the deep ocean — pressure, light, life, and the limits of human reach.
© MMXXV · Bathyal Press · Compiled from oceanographic public records.

visibleText: 0 1k 2k 3k 4k 5k 6k 7k 8k 9k 10k 11k HADAL · CARTOGRAPHY Pressure Zones Creatures Light Layers Human Reach Data Dive CHAPTER 03 / PHOTIC PENETRATION Light Layers Sunlight does not descend uniformly. Each wavelength is filtered, absorbed, and scattered at a different rate. By 200 meters, the visible spectrum has collapsed to a faint, monochrome blue — and beyond, only darkness, broken solely by bioluminescent code. PHOTIC 0 – 200 m · SUNLIT DYSPHOTIC 200 – 1000 m · TWILIGHT APHOTIC 1000 m+ · MIDNIGHT RED · 5m ORANGE · 25m YELLOW · 50m GREEN · 75m BLUE · 200m § 3.1 — SPECTRAL ATTENUATION How wavelengths die in water Water acts as a selective filter. Long-wavelength reds are absorbed within meters of the surface; cooler, shorter wavelengths persist deeper, until only a narrow band of blue-green photons reach the threshold of the dysphotic zone at 200 meters. 700 nm ↓ 5 m Red 620 nm ↓ 25 m Orange 580 nm ↓ 50 m Yellow 530 nm ↓ 75 m Green 470 nm ↓ 200 m Blue § 3.2 — VISIBLE SPECTRUM, MASKED The ocean-penetrating window Of the full electromagnetic spectrum, only a narrow visible band ever reaches beyond surface waters. Below, the visible strip is rendered, then masked through an SVG <clipPath> to isolate the 470–700 nm range that meaningfully penetrates seawater. 380nm 440 500 580 660 740nm ◀ UV IR ▶ OCEAN-PENETRATING WINDOW · 470–700 nm 200m 75m 50m 25m 5m MAX PENETRATION DEPTH PER WAVELENGTH § 3.3 — PHOTON BUDGET Key thresholds 1% Surface light at 100m The 1% threshold conventionally defines the lower bound of the photic (euphotic) zone where photosynthesis remains viable. 200m Photic / Dysphotic boundary Below this depth, sunlight is insufficient to drive photosynthesis. Only blue-green photons penetrate further. 1000m Dysphotic / Aphotic boundary Below 1 km, no measurable sunlight remains. The only light is biological — bioluminescent flashes from resident fauna. 90% Of the ocean is aphotic By volume, nearly the entire ocean exists in permanent darkness. The sunlit surface is a thin, exceptional film.
